faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Pelham Bay?
faqs.answer: Pelham Bay's location near wooded parkland, mature trees throughout the neighborhood, and many older homes with wooden structures create perfect conditions for carpenter ants. These pests are attracted to moisture-damaged wood, which is common in the area's aging housing stock, particularly in properties built before the 1970s.

faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Pelham Bay?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Pelham Bay typically ranges from $350 to $2,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, colony location, and property size. Single-family homes generally require more extensive treatment than apartments, and multiple colonies or hard-to-reach nests may increase costs. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning treatment.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants (typically ¼ to ½ inch long) and are usually black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards or windowsills, rustling sounds inside walls, and winged ants appearing indoors in spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants have a narrow waist and bent antennae.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take in Pelham Bay homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 1-2 hours depending on your property size and infestation severity. Most infestations require follow-up visits over 2-4 weeks to ensure complete colony elimination. Our technicians will locate nests, apply targeted treatments to affected areas, and create exterior barriers to prevent re-infestation, with results usually visible within the first week.
